result.stdout:
Examples:
  # Display all the action for all WorkflowGen request
    wfg action get

  # Display all the action for the request number 100
    wfg action get 100

  # Display all open action for the request number 100
    wfg action get 100 -s OPEN

  # Display all the action of the process SIMPLE_REQUEST version 1
    wfg action get -p SIMPLE_REQUEST -v 1

  # Display all the action for all WorkflowGen request in a JSON format
    wfg action get 100 --output json

  # Display all the action of the process SIMPLE_REQUEST version 1 in a JSON format
    wfg action get -p SIMPLE_REQUEST -v 1 --output json


get:
  Lets you display the action information of a request or process

Usage:
  wfg action get [options] [<requestNumber>]

Arguments:
  <requestNumber>    Id number of request which you want to get

Options:
  -p, --processName <processName>          Name of the process which you want to retrieve the actions
  -v, --processVersion <processVersion>    Version of the process which you want to retrieve the actions
  -s, --status <status>                    Retrieve the requests with the status specified, OPEN or CLOSED
  -o, --output <output>                    Define the output format. Valid values: json, table. [default: table]
  --debug                                  Show debug log
  --verbose                                Show verbose log
  -?, -h, --help                           Show help and usage information

config:

Usage:
  wfg config [options] [command]

Options:
  --debug           Show debug log
  --verbose         Show verbose log
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  current-context                         Displays the current context
  delete-context <name>                   Delete the specified context from the configuration
  delete-server <name>                    Delete the specified server from the configuration
  delete-user <name>                      Delete the specified user from the configuration
  get-contexts <names>                    Describe one or many contexts
  get-servers                             Display servers defined in the configuration
  get-users                               Display users defined in the configuration
  rename-context <old-name> <new-name>    Renames a context from the configuration
  rename-server <old-name> <new-name>     Renames a server from the configuration
  rename-user <old-name> <new-name>       Renames a user from the configuration
  set-context <name>                      Sets a context entry in the configuration
  set-server <name>                       Sets a server entry in configuration
  set-user <name>                         Sets a user entry in the configuration
  use-context <name>                      Sets the current-context in the configuration

  # Connect to your WorkflowGen instance with basic authentication, with the wfgen_admin account and the YourPassword password
    wfg login https://DOMAIN/wfgen --username wfgen_admin --password YourPassword

    wfg login https://DOMAIN/wfgen --username wfgen_admin --password-file ./path/to/password.txt

  # Connect to your WorkflowGen instance with basic authentication, with the wfgen_admin account and the YourPassword password, and define it as DEV context
    wfg login https://DOMAIN/wfgen --username wfgen_admin --password YourPassword --context DEV

  # Connect to your WorkflowGen instance with Auth0
    wfg login https://DOMAIN/wfgen --authentication-mode AUTH0 --domain YourDomain.auth0.com --client-id YourClientId 

  # Connect to your WorkflowGen instance with Auth0 with client credentials
    wfg login https://DOMAIN/wfgen --authentication-mode AUTH0 --domain YourDomain.auth0.com --client-id YourClientId --client-secret YourClientSecret

  # Connect to your WorkflowGen instance with Microsoft Identity V2
    wfg login https://DOMAIN/wfgen --authentication-mode MSIDV2 --tenant-id YourTenantId --client-id YourClientId

  # Connect to your WorkflowGen instance with Microsoft Identity V2 with client credentials
    wfg login https://DOMAIN/wfgen --authentication-mode MSIDV2 --tenant-id YourTenantId --client-id YourClientId --client-secret YourClientSecret

  # Connect to your WorkflowGen instance with Okta
    wfg login https://DOMAIN/wfgen --authentication-mode OKTA --metadata-url https://YourOktaInformation/.well-known/openid-configuration --client-id YourClientId

  # Connect to your WorkflowGen instance with Okta with client credentials
    wfg login https://DOMAIN/wfgen --authentication-mode OKTA --metadata-url https://YourOktaInformation/.well-known/openid-configuration --client-id YourClientId --client-secret YourClientSecret

  # Connect to your WorkflowGen instance with AD FS
    wfg login https://DOMAIN/wfgen --authentication-mode ADFS --domain YourAdfsDomain --client-id YourClientId

  # Connect to your WorkflowGen instance with AD FS with client credentials
    wfg login https://DOMAIN/wfgen --authentication-mode ADFS --domain YourAdfsDomain --client-id YourClientId --client-secret YourClientSecret


login:
  Lets you configure your current WorkflowGen configuration (credentials, server)

Usage:
  wfg login [options] <url>

Arguments:
  <url>    WorkflowGen Url (ex. https://example.com/wfgen)

Options:
  -u, --username <username>                      WorkflowGen username
  -p, --password <password>                      WorkflowGen password
  --password-file <password-file>                WorkflowGen password file path
  -w, --webforms <webforms>                      WorkflowGen webforms path [default: C:\inetpub\wwwroot\wfgen\wfapps\webforms]
  -c, --context <context>                        Name of the context where the configuration will be used. If the context already exists, it will be replaced. Otherwise, it will be created [default: DEFAULT]
  --authentication-mode <authentication-mode>    (BASIC, AUTH0, OKTA, MSIDV2, ADFS) [default: BASIC]
  --client-id <client-id>                        The client id is a public identifier
  --domain <domain>                              The domain for ADFS or AUTH0
  --tenant-id <tenant-id>                        The tenant id for MSIDV2
  --metadata-url <metadata-url>                  The meta data URL for OKTA
  --client-secret <client-secret>                The client secret is a secret known only to the application and the authorization server
  --client-secret-file <client-secret-file>      The client secret file is a file containing a client secret known only to the application and the authorization server
  --port <port>                                  Your WorkflowGen port [default: 8888]
  --debug                                        Show debug log
  --verbose                                      Show verbose log
  -?, -h, --help                                 Show help and usage information

  # Display information for all open WorkflowGen request  in a JSON format
    wfg request get -s OPEN --ouput json

  # Display request 100 information in a JSON format
    wfg request get 100 --output json

  # Display all requests from the process SIMPLE_REQUEST in a JSON format
    wfg request get -p SIMPLE_REQUEST 

  # Display all closed requests from the process SIMPLE_REQUEST in a JSON format
    wfg request get -p SIMPLE_REQUEST -s CLOSED --output json

  # Display all requests from the process SIMPLE_REQUEST version 1 in a JSON format
    wfg request get -p SIMPLE_REQUEST -v 1 --output json


get:
  Lets you display one or several request information

Usage:
  wfg request get [options] [<requestNumbers>...]

Arguments:
  <requestNumbers>    Id number(s) of request(s) which you want to get

Options:
  -p, --processName <processName>          Name of the process which you want to retrieve requests
  -v, --processVersion <processVersion>    Version of the process which you want to retrieve requests
  -s, --status <status>                    Retrieve the requests with the status specified, OPEN or CLOSED
  -o, --output <output>                    Define the output format. Valid values: json, table. [default: table]
  --debug                                  Show debug log
  --verbose                                Show verbose log
  -?, -h, --help                           Show help and usage information
